prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene on a PolarPlane where a rose curve morphs as its frequency changes. Use a live ValueTracker readout, staged captions, and clear transition beats so the petal count change is easy to follow.
completion:
from manim import *

class PolarFrequencyMorphStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Frequency Changes the Rose Curve", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = PolarPlane(radius_max=3.5, azimuth_units="PI radians").add_coordinates().scale(0.95).shift(LEFT * 1.5 + DOWN * 0.15)
        tracker = ValueTracker(3.0)
        graph = always_redraw(lambda: plane.plot_polar_graph(lambda theta: 2.1 * np.sin(tracker.get_value() * theta), theta_range=[0, TAU], color=ORANGE, stroke_width=5))
        freq_panel = always_redraw(lambda: VGroup(Text("frequency", font_size=23, color=GRAY_D), DecimalNumber(tracker.get_value(), num_decimal_places=1, font_size=30, color=ORANGE)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.14).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 1.1))
        petals_text = always_redraw(lambda: Text(f"visual petals: about {int(round(2 * tracker.get_value())) if int(round(tracker.get_value())) % 2 == 0 else int(round(tracker.get_value()))}", font_size=22, color=TEAL_D).next_to(freq_panel, DOWN, buff=0.22))
        caption_a = Text("Low frequencies create simpler polar flowers.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("As the frequency rises, the curve loops more often around the pole.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The same polar rule can morph smoothly into a much richer pattern.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), FadeIn(freq_panel), FadeIn(petals_text), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(graph), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(4.0), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=6)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(5.0), run_time=5)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(6.0), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=6)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(4.5), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that compares divergence and curl using animated stream lines. Start with an outward field, then transition to a rotating field, with labels and a small comparison table that updates across the scene.
completion:
from manim import *

class StreamlineCurlDivergenceStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Divergence Versus Curl", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.28}).shift(LEFT * 1.9 + DOWN * 0.15)
        divergence = StreamLines(lambda p: 0.35 * np.array([p[0], p[1], 0.0]), x_range=[-4, 4, 0.8], y_range=[-3, 3, 0.8], stroke_width=2.0, max_anchors_per_line=24)
        curl = StreamLines(lambda p: 0.45 * np.array([-p[1], p[0], 0.0]), x_range=[-4, 4, 0.8], y_range=[-3, 3, 0.8], stroke_width=2.0, max_anchors_per_line=24)
        field_label = Text("divergence", font_size=26, color=BLUE_D).next_to(plane, UP)
        table = MathTable([["field", "behavior"], ["divergence", "spreads outward"], ["curl", "rotates around"], ["shared note", "both are vector fields"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.34).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(DOWN * 0.1)
        row_div = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[1], color=BLUE_D, buff=0.05)
        row_curl = SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[2], color=ORANGE, buff=0.05)
        caption_a = Text("Divergence sends nearby trajectories away from one another.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Curl bends the flow into local rotation instead of pure expansion.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("A comparison table makes the behavioral contrast explicit.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), FadeIn(field_label), FadeIn(table), Create(row_div), run_time=4)
        self.add(divergence)
        divergence.start_animation(warm_up=False, flow_speed=1.35)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=3)
        self.wait(4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(row_div, row_curl), field_label.animate.become(Text("curl", font_size=26, color=ORANGE).next_to(plane, UP)), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=3)
        self.remove(divergence)
        self.add(curl)
        curl.start_animation(warm_up=False, flow_speed=1.35)
        self.wait(5)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that starts with a discrete probability mass display and transitions toward a smooth bell-shaped approximation. Use animated bars, a curve overlay, and explanatory captions so the discrete-to-continuous idea is clear.
completion:
from manim import *

class ProbabilityMassToCurveStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("From Probability Mass to a Smooth Curve", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        chart = BarChart(values=[0.03, 0.08, 0.16, 0.24, 0.21, 0.16, 0.08, 0.04], bar_names=["0", "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7"], y_range=[0, 0.3, 0.05], y_length=3.8, x_length=6.4).shift(LEFT * 2.0 + DOWN * 0.2)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 7, 1], y_range=[0, 0.3, 0.05], x_length=6.4, y_length=3.8, tips=False).move_to(chart)
        curve = axes.plot(lambda x: 0.24 * np.exp(-((x - 3.5) ** 2) / 3.0), x_range=[0, 7], color=ORANGE, stroke_width=5)
        area = axes.get_area(curve, x_range=[0.3, 6.7], color=ORANGE, opacity=0.16)
        connector = Arrow(chart.get_right() + RIGHT * 0.15, axes.get_left() + LEFT * 0.15, color=GRAY_B)
        caption_a = Text("Discrete probabilities assign mass to separate outcomes.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("As the pattern smooths out, a continuous approximation becomes useful.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The bars and curve tell the same story at different levels of granularity.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        guide = SurroundingRectangle(chart.bars[3], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.08)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(chart), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(guide), Indicate(chart.bars[3]), Indicate(chart.bars[4]), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(connector), FadeIn(axes), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.play(TransformFromCopy(chart.bars, curve), FadeIn(area), FadeOut(guide), run_time=6)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=3)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that explains residual signal flow through three blocks. Use LaggedStart and AnimationGroup to reveal nodes, arrows, and a highlighted skip path in separate beats with clear transition captions.
completion:
from manim import *

class LaggedStartResidualSignalStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Residual Flow Builds on Earlier Signals", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        blocks = VGroup(*[RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.16, width=1.6, height=1.0, color=BLUE_D) for _ in range(3)]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=1.0).shift(UP * 0.3)
        labels = VGroup(Text("block 1", font_size=24), Text("block 2", font_size=24), Text("block 3", font_size=24))
        for label, block in zip(labels, blocks):
            label.move_to(block)
        arrows = VGroup(*[Arrow(blocks[i].get_right(), blocks[i + 1].get_left(), buff=0.12, color=GRAY_B, stroke_width=4) for i in range(2)])
        input_dot = Dot(blocks[0].get_left() + LEFT * 0.8, color=YELLOW, radius=0.09)
        output_dot = Dot(blocks[-1].get_right() + RIGHT * 0.8, color=TEAL_D, radius=0.09)
        input_arrow = Arrow(input_dot.get_right(), blocks[0].get_left(), buff=0.12, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        output_arrow = Arrow(blocks[-1].get_right(), output_dot.get_left(), buff=0.12, color=TEAL_D, stroke_width=4)
        skip_path = CubicBezier(blocks[0].get_top() + UP * 0.2, blocks[0].get_top() + UP * 1.0, blocks[-1].get_top() + UP * 1.0, blocks[-1].get_top() + UP * 0.2).set_stroke(color=ORANGE, width=5)
        skip_label = Text("skip path", font_size=22, color=ORANGE).next_to(skip_path, UP, buff=0.1)
        merge_box = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(blocks[-1], labels[-1]), color=ORANGE, buff=0.18)
        caption_a = Text("The main path processes the signal block by block.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A residual path carries earlier information around the stack.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("At the end, both paths contribute to the final representation.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(input_dot), FadeIn(output_dot), run_time=3)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(block) for block in blocks], *[Write(label) for label in labels], lag_ratio=0.15), run_time=4)
        self.play(AnimationGroup(GrowArrow(input_arrow), GrowArrow(arrows[0]), GrowArrow(arrows[1]), GrowArrow(output_arrow), lag_ratio=0.2), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(Create(skip_path), FadeIn(skip_label), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=5)
        self.play(Create(merge_box), Indicate(skip_path), Indicate(arrows[-1]), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=5)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene where a packet travels through a staged route graph. Use Succession and MoveAlongPath so the packet traverses one leg after another, with a traced path and captions that explain each transition.
completion:
from manim import *

class SuccessionPacketRouteStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Packet Traverses the Route in Stages", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        points = {
            "A": LEFT * 4 + UP * 1.5,
            "B": LEFT * 1.6 + UP * 1.0,
            "C": RIGHT * 0.4 + UP * 1.8,
            "D": RIGHT * 2.5 + UP * 0.3,
            "E": RIGHT * 0.2 + DOWN * 1.4,
        }
        nodes = VGroup(*[Dot(pos, color=BLUE_D, radius=0.09) for pos in points.values()])
        node_labels = VGroup(*[Text(name, font_size=24).next_to(Dot(pos), UP if pos[1] >= 0 else DOWN) for name, pos in points.items()])
        leg1 = VMobject(color=YELLOW_D).set_points_smoothly([points['A'], points['B'], points['C']])
        leg2 = VMobject(color=YELLOW_D).set_points_smoothly([points['C'], points['D']])
        leg3 = VMobject(color=YELLOW_D).set_points_smoothly([points['D'], points['E']])
        route = VGroup(leg1.copy().set_stroke(color=GRAY_B, width=4), leg2.copy().set_stroke(color=GRAY_B, width=4), leg3.copy().set_stroke(color=GRAY_B, width=4))
        packet = Dot(points['A'], color=YELLOW, radius=0.1)
        trace = TracedPath(packet.get_center, stroke_color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        caption_a = Text("The first leg aggregates local context before the handoff.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A second transition carries the packet into the high-priority node.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("The final leg delivers the packet to the sink node.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.add(trace)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(nodes), Write(node_labels), Create(route), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(packet), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=2)
        self.play(Succession(MoveAlongPath(packet, leg1), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), MoveAlongPath(packet, leg2), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), MoveAlongPath(packet, leg3)), run_time=11)
        self.play(Indicate(nodes[-1]), run_time=2)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that starts from a symbolic formula and transitions into a compact comparison table. Use TransformMatchingTex and FadeTransform so the structure of the expression carries over into the summary view.
completion:
from manim import *

class TransformMatchingFormulaTableStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Formula Can Collapse into a Summary Table", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        formula = MathTex("f(x)", "=", "a x^2", "+", "b x", "+", "c").scale(1.4).shift(UP * 1.0)
        colored_formula = MathTex("f(x)", "=", "a x^2", "+", "b x", "+", "c").scale(1.4).move_to(formula)
        colored_formula[2].set_color(BLUE_D)
        colored_formula[4].set_color(ORANGE)
        colored_formula[6].set_color(TEAL_D)
        labels = VGroup(Text("curvature", font_size=24, color=BLUE_D), Text("slope", font_size=24, color=ORANGE), Text("offset", font_size=24, color=TEAL_D)).arrange(DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.28).to_edge(LEFT).shift(DOWN * 0.5)
        table = MathTable([["term", "role"], ["a x^2", "curvature"], ["b x", "slope"], ["c", "offset"]], include_outer_lines=True).scale(0.58).shift(DOWN * 0.15)
        row_boxes = VGroup(*[SurroundingRectangle(table.get_rows()[i], color=color, buff=0.05) for i, color in zip([1, 2, 3], [BLUE_D, ORANGE, TEAL_D])])
        caption_a = Text("The symbolic view shows the structure directly in the formula.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Color and matching transitions preserve the role of each term.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("A summary table trades algebraic detail for a faster conceptual readout.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Write(formula), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(TransformMatchingTex(formula, colored_formula), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(label) for label in labels], lag_ratio=0.2), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=5)
        self.play(FadeTransform(VGroup(colored_formula, labels), table), run_time=5)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[Create(box) for box in row_boxes], lag_ratio=0.18), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=4)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that compares a slow orbit and a fast orbit around the same center. Use ChangeSpeed to vary pacing during the explanation, include staged captions, and make the transition in tempo visually obvious.
completion:
from manim import *

class ChangeSpeedOrbitComparisonStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Speed Changes Can Reshape Attention", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        left_orbit = Circle(radius=1.2, color=BLUE_D).shift(LEFT * 2.5 + DOWN * 0.2)
        right_orbit = Circle(radius=1.2, color=ORANGE).shift(RIGHT * 2.5 + DOWN * 0.2)
        left_dot = Dot(left_orbit.point_at_angle(0), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        right_dot = Dot(right_orbit.point_at_angle(0), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        left_label = Text("slow orbit", font_size=24, color=BLUE_D).next_to(left_orbit, UP)
        right_label = Text("tempo shift", font_size=24, color=ORANGE).next_to(right_orbit, UP)
        center_line = DashedLine(left_orbit.get_center(), right_orbit.get_center(), color=GRAY_B)
        caption_a = Text("Both paths trace the same geometry.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("But a speed modifier changes where the viewer spends attention.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("Tempo is part of the explanation, not just decoration.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(left_orbit), Create(right_orbit), FadeIn(left_label), FadeIn(right_label), Create(center_line), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(left_dot), FadeIn(right_dot), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=2)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(left_dot, left_orbit), ChangeSpeed(MoveAlongPath(right_dot, right_orbit), speedinfo={0.0: 0.5, 0.45: 1.7, 0.8: 0.8, 1.0: 1.2}), run_time=8)
        self.play(Indicate(right_label), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=3)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(left_dot, left_orbit), ChangeSpeed(MoveAlongPath(right_dot, right_orbit), speedinfo={0.0: 1.5, 0.35: 0.7, 0.7: 1.8, 1.0: 0.6}), run_time=7)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 30-second Manim scene that replaces one representation with another in stages. Start from colored blocks, then use ReplacementTransform to turn them into a bar chart and finally into a labeled summary view with transition captions.
completion:
from manim import *

class ReplacementTransformRepresentationStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Representation Can Be Rewritten in Stages", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        cards = VGroup(*[RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=1.1, height=1.6, color=color, fill_opacity=0.25) for color in [BLUE_D, ORANGE, TEAL_D]]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.45).shift(UP * 0.2 + LEFT * 2.2)
        card_labels = VGroup(Text("signal", font_size=22, color=BLUE_D), Text("weight", font_size=22, color=ORANGE), Text("score", font_size=22, color=TEAL_D))
        for label, card in zip(card_labels, cards):
            label.move_to(card)
        chart = BarChart(values=[2.4, 1.8, 3.2], bar_names=["signal", "weight", "score"], y_range=[0, 4, 1], y_length=3.6, x_length=4.8).shift(RIGHT * 2.4 + DOWN * 0.2)
        summary = VGroup(Text("highest: score", font_size=26, color=TEAL_D), Text("lowest: weight", font_size=26, color=ORANGE), Text("signal stays in the middle", font_size=26, color=BLUE_D)).arrange(DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.28).move_to(chart)
        summary_box = SurroundingRectangle(summary[0], color=TEAL_D, buff=0.12)
        caption_a = Text("The starting cards give a discrete categorical view.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A bar chart keeps the same entities but shifts attention to magnitude.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("A final summary strips the view down to the key comparison.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(card) for card in cards], *[Write(label) for label in card_labels], lag_ratio=0.15), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(VGroup(cards, card_labels), chart), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=5)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(chart, summary), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=5)
        self.play(Create(summary_box), Indicate(summary[0]), run_time=3)
        self.wait(3)

prompt: Create a 20-second Manim scene that starts from a curve with a highlighted peak, then uses FadeTransform to turn the live plot into a compact summary card. Include transition captions so the shift from detailed view to takeaway is explicit.
completion:
from manim import *

class FadeTransformPeakSummaryStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("From Detailed Plot to Takeaway Card", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 10, 1], y_range=[0, 7, 1], x_length=7.0, y_length=4.2, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 1.1 + DOWN * 0.2)
        graph = axes.plot(lambda x: 0.8 + 0.28 * x + 2.2 * np.exp(-((x - 6.2) ** 2) / 0.55), x_range=[0.5, 9.4], color=BLUE_D)
        peak_dot = Dot(axes.c2p(6.2, 4.74), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        peak_box = SurroundingRectangle(VGroup(peak_dot), color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.16)
        card = VGroup(Text("key takeaway", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D), Text("peak near x = 6.2", font_size=28, color=YELLOW_D), Text("local maximum dominates the story", font_size=24, color=BLUE_D)).arrange(DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT, buff=0.22).move_to(RIGHT * 2.8 + DOWN * 0.1)
        card_box = SurroundingRectangle(card, color=TEAL_D, buff=0.18)
        caption_a = Text("The full plot provides detailed context.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Highlighting the peak focuses the viewer on the critical feature.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_c = Text("A summary card then condenses the same insight into a takeaway.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(graph), FadeIn(caption_a), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(peak_dot), Create(peak_box), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeTransform(VGroup(axes, graph, peak_dot, peak_box), VGroup(card, card_box)), ReplacementTransform(caption_b, caption_c), run_time=4)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 20-second ThreeDScene with a colored surface and a highlighted probe point. Use camera orientation changes and a short ambient rotation so the viewer first sees the surface globally and then from an angled flyover view.
completion:
from manim import *

class SurfaceCameraFlyoverStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Camera Flyover Reveals Surface Shape", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-2, 3, 1])
        surface = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, v, 0.18 * (u ** 2 - v ** 2)), u_range=[-2.2, 2.2], v_range=[-2.2, 2.2], checkerboard_colors=[BLUE_D, TEAL_D], resolution=(16, 16), fill_opacity=0.75)
        probe = Dot3D(axes.c2p(1.4, -0.9, 0.18 * (1.4 ** 2 - (-0.9) ** 2)), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        probe_line = DashedLine(axes.c2p(1.4, -0.9, 0), probe.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D)
        caption_a = Text("Start with a wide camera to see the whole saddle surface.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Then tilt and rotate to reveal the rise and fall from a side angle.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=65 * DEGREES, theta=-40 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(axes), FadeIn(surface), FadeIn(probe), Create(probe_line), run_time=3)
        self.wait(1)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=2)
        self.move_camera(phi=78 * DEGREES, theta=25 * DEGREES, run_time=4)
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.15)
        self.wait(4)
        self.stop_ambient_camera_rotation()
        self.move_camera(phi=62 * DEGREES, theta=-55 * DEGREES, run_time=3)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second ThreeDScene with a helical curve and a point moving along it. Use one camera transition to switch from a side perspective to a top-down perspective while the point continues moving.
completion:
from manim import *

class HelixCameraTransitionStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Perspective Changes How a Helix Feels", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-1, 5, 1])
        helix = ParametricFunction(lambda t: axes.c2p(1.6 * np.cos(t), 1.6 * np.sin(t), 0.35 * t), t_range=[0, 3.5 * TAU], color=ORANGE, stroke_width=5)
        traveler = Dot3D(helix.get_start(), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        trace = TracedPath(traveler.get_center, stroke_color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=3)
        caption_a = Text("From the side, the helix reads as a climbing spiral.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("From above, the same path collapses toward a circular trace.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=70 * DEGREES, theta=-55 * DEGREES)
        self.add(trace)
        self.play(FadeIn(axes), Create(helix), FadeIn(traveler), run_time=3)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(traveler, helix), run_time=5, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=2)
        self.move_camera(phi=15 * DEGREES, theta=-90 * DEGREES, run_time=4)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(traveler, helix), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second ThreeDScene that starts with flat rectangles, transforms them into a stacked 3D prism arrangement, and then moves the camera to show the depth structure from a different angle.
completion:
from manim import *

class PrismStackCameraStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Camera Shift Reveals Hidden Depth", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        flat_cards = VGroup(*[Rectangle(width=1.6, height=0.8, color=color, fill_opacity=0.28) for color in [BLUE_D, ORANGE, TEAL_D]]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.25).shift(LEFT * 2.5)
        prisms = VGroup(
            Prism(dimensions=[1.6, 0.8, 0.5], fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.65, stroke_color=BLUE_E).shift(LEFT * 1.2 + DOWN * 0.7),
            Prism(dimensions=[1.6, 0.8, 0.8], fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.65, stroke_color=RED_E).shift(LEFT * 0.1),
            Prism(dimensions=[1.6, 0.8, 1.2], fill_color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.65, stroke_color=TEAL_E).shift(RIGHT * 1.2 + UP * 0.8),
        )
        caption_a = Text("The flat view hides the different heights.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Once the rectangles become prisms, camera angle matters.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=68 * DEGREES, theta=-40 * DEGREES)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(card) for card in flat_cards], lag_ratio=0.18), run_time=3)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[ReplacementTransform(card, prism) for card, prism in zip(flat_cards, prisms)], lag_ratio=0.18), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.move_camera(phi=75 * DEGREES, theta=18 * DEGREES, run_time=4)
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.12)
        self.wait(3)
        self.stop_ambient_camera_rotation()
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second ThreeDScene that begins with several contour-like cross-section curves on a plane and then transitions into the full surface they came from. Use a camera movement and staged captions to make the 2D-to-3D transition clear.
completion:
from manim import *

class SurfaceContourTransitionStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Contours Hint at the Surface Above Them", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-1, 3, 1])
        contour_levels = [-1.2, 0.0, 1.2]
        contours = VGroup(*[
            ParametricFunction(lambda t, y=y: axes.c2p(t, y, 0.0), t_range=[-2.2, 2.2], color=color, stroke_width=5)
            for y, color in zip(contour_levels, [BLUE_D, YELLOW_D, ORANGE])
        ])
        surface = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, v, 0.22 * np.sin(u) + 0.16 * v), u_range=[-2.4, 2.4], v_range=[-2.0, 2.0], checkerboard_colors=[TEAL_D, BLUE_D], resolution=(16, 16), fill_opacity=0.72)
        caption_a = Text("Cross-sections give a flat preview of changing height.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Tilting the camera and adding the surface reveals the full geometry.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=70 * DEGREES, theta=-90 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(axes), LaggedStart(*[Create(curve) for curve in contours], lag_ratio=0.18), run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=2)
        self.move_camera(phi=68 * DEGREES, theta=-38 * DEGREES, run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(surface), run_time=4)
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.1)
        self.wait(4)
        self.stop_ambient_camera_rotation()
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second ThreeDScene with a sphere, a moving point on a latitude circle, and a projected shadow onto a plane below. Use camera motion and transition captions so the relationship between the 3D point and its projection is easy to follow.
completion:
from manim import *

class SphereProjectionOrbitStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Projection Links 3D Motion to a Flat Shadow", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-2, 3, 1])
        sphere = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(1.4 * np.cos(u) * np.cos(v), 1.4 * np.cos(u) * np.sin(v), 1.4 * np.sin(u)), u_range=[-PI / 2, PI / 2], v_range=[0, TAU], checkerboard_colors=[BLUE_D, BLUE_E], resolution=(14, 20), fill_opacity=0.5)
        orbit = ParametricFunction(lambda t: axes.c2p(1.2 * np.cos(t), 1.2 * np.sin(t), 0.7), t_range=[0, TAU], color=ORANGE, stroke_width=4)
        tracker = ValueTracker(0.0)
        point = always_redraw(lambda: Dot3D(axes.c2p(1.2 * np.cos(tracker.get_value()), 1.2 * np.sin(tracker.get_value()), 0.7), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08))
        shadow = always_redraw(lambda: Dot3D(axes.c2p(1.2 * np.cos(tracker.get_value()), 1.2 * np.sin(tracker.get_value()), -1.1), color=TEAL_D, radius=0.07))
        link = always_redraw(lambda: DashedLine(point.get_center(), shadow.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=3))
        plane = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, v, -1.1), u_range=[-2.1, 2.1], v_range=[-2.1, 2.1], checkerboard_colors=[GRAY_D, GRAY_E], resolution=(2, 2), fill_opacity=0.22)
        caption_a = Text("The moving point lives on a raised orbit around the sphere.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Its shadow on the plane below preserves the horizontal motion.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=68 * DEGREES, theta=-42 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(axes), FadeIn(plane), FadeIn(sphere), Create(orbit), FadeIn(point), FadeIn(shadow), Create(link), run_time=4)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(PI), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=2)
        self.move_camera(phi=60 * DEGREES, theta=18 * DEGREES, run_time=3)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(TAU), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *

class LayeredRibbonCameraStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Camera Motion Separates Layered Ribbons", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-2, 2, 1])
        ribbon_a = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, 0.65 * np.sin(u) + v, 0.32 * u), u_range=[-2.2, 2.2], v_range=[-0.25, 0.25], checkerboard_colors=[BLUE_D, TEAL_D], resolution=(18, 4), fill_opacity=0.72)
        ribbon_b = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, -0.65 * np.sin(u) + v, -0.32 * u), u_range=[-2.2, 2.2], v_range=[-0.25, 0.25], checkerboard_colors=[ORANGE, RED_D], resolution=(18, 4), fill_opacity=0.68)
        label_a = Text("layer A", font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).to_corner(UL).shift(DOWN * 0.6)
        label_b = Text("layer B", font_size=22, color=ORANGE).next_to(label_a, DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        caption_a = Text("From one angle the two ribbons appear tightly entangled.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A camera shift separates the layers and makes the crossing structure legible.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(label_a, label_b, caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=72 * DEGREES, theta=-58 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(axes), FadeIn(ribbon_a), FadeIn(ribbon_b), FadeIn(label_a), FadeIn(label_b), run_time=4)
        self.move_camera(phi=68 * DEGREES, theta=8 * DEGREES, run_time=4)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=2)
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.12)
        self.wait(4)
        self.stop_ambient_camera_rotation()
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *

class PlaneToSurfaceMorphStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Flat Plane Can Morph into Curved Geometry", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-2, 3, 1])
        plane = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, v, 0), u_range=[-2.2, 2.2], v_range=[-2.2, 2.2], checkerboard_colors=[GRAY_D, GRAY_E], resolution=(10, 10), fill_opacity=0.45)
        curved = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, v, 0.2 * np.sin(u) * np.cos(v) + 0.1 * u), u_range=[-2.2, 2.2], v_range=[-2.2, 2.2], checkerboard_colors=[BLUE_D, TEAL_D], resolution=(14, 14), fill_opacity=0.72)
        caption_a = Text("Start from a flat reference surface.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("Then warp it and change the camera so the height variation becomes obvious.", font_size=22, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=70 * DEGREES, theta=-88 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(axes), FadeIn(plane), run_time=3)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(plane, curved), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.move_camera(phi=68 * DEGREES, theta=-35 * DEGREES, run_time=4)
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.1)
        self.wait(3)
        self.stop_ambient_camera_rotation()
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *

class ConeSliceCameraStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("A Slice Explains the Cone from the Inside", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-3, 3, 1], z_range=[-1, 4, 1])
        cone = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(v * np.cos(u), v * np.sin(u), 2.2 - v), u_range=[0, TAU], v_range=[0, 2.0], checkerboard_colors=[ORANGE, RED_D], resolution=(18, 12), fill_opacity=0.6)
        slice_plane = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p(u, v, 1.1), u_range=[-1.3, 1.3], v_range=[-1.3, 1.3], checkerboard_colors=[TEAL_D, TEAL_E], resolution=(2, 2), fill_opacity=0.25)
        slice_circle = Circle(radius=1.1, color=YELLOW_D).move_to(axes.c2p(0, 0, 1.1))
        caption_a = Text("The full cone shows the global 3D shape.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A horizontal slice exposes a circular cross-section inside the cone.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=72 * DEGREES, theta=-48 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(axes), FadeIn(cone), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(slice_plane), Create(slice_circle), ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=4)
        self.move_camera(phi=60 * DEGREES, theta=20 * DEGREES, run_time=4)
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.1)
        self.wait(3)
        self.stop_ambient_camera_rotation()
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 20-second ThreeDScene with a torus-like surface and a highlighted orbit running around it. Use camera movement while a point travels the orbit so the viewer sees both the local loop and the global doughnut shape.
completion:
from manim import *

class TorusOrbitCameraStory(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Camera Motion Clarifies the Torus Orbit", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(title)
        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-4, 4, 1], z_range=[-2, 2, 1])
        torus = Surface(lambda u, v: axes.c2p((2.0 + 0.55 * np.cos(v)) * np.cos(u), (2.0 + 0.55 * np.cos(v)) * np.sin(u), 0.55 * np.sin(v)), u_range=[0, TAU], v_range=[0, TAU], checkerboard_colors=[BLUE_D, BLUE_E], resolution=(20, 12), fill_opacity=0.6)
        orbit = ParametricFunction(lambda t: axes.c2p((2.0 + 0.55 * np.cos(0.4 * PI)) * np.cos(t), (2.0 + 0.55 * np.cos(0.4 * PI)) * np.sin(t), 0.55 * np.sin(0.4 * PI)), t_range=[0, TAU], color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        traveler = Dot3D(orbit.get_start(), color=YELLOW, radius=0.08)
        caption_a = Text("The orbit circles one tube of the torus.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_b = Text("A camera move helps separate the local loop from the global torus shape.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(caption_a)

        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=68 * DEGREES, theta=-45 * DEGREES)
        self.play(FadeIn(axes), FadeIn(torus), Create(orbit), FadeIn(traveler), run_time=4)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(traveler, orbit), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(caption_a, caption_b), run_time=2)
        self.move_camera(phi=58 * DEGREES, theta=18 * DEGREES, run_time=4)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(traveler, orbit), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(1)
